summary: Matched a batch of previously stubbed functions in src/melee/gm/gm_1B03.c, mostly small game-mode scene OnLoad handlers, scene transition helpers, video/randomization setup helpers, and MTHP/DVD cleanup callbacks. The PR also tightened gm_1B03.h prototypes from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to concrete void or MinorScene signatures, and adjusted related vi module prototypes and data types so gm_1B03 could call them cleanly. Evidence is primarily from the diff; the PR body/review excerpts contain no substantive explanation or reviewer feedback.
what_changed: Implemented gm_801B23C4_OnLoad to initialize extern bytes gm_804D68C0 and gm_804D68C1, assigning gm_804D68C0 from gm_801677F0() and clearing gm_804D68C1.
Implemented gm_801B2510(MinorScene*) by reading scene data via gm_801A4284 and calling gm_801A42F8(1) when the loaded value is 1 or 2.

Implemented repeated simple OnLoad handlers such as gm_801B8D88_OnLoad, gm_801B8F98_OnLoad, gm_801B91A8_OnLoad, gm_801BA0EC_OnLoad, gm_801BA2EC_OnLoad, gm_801BA4EC_OnLoad, gm_801BA6E4_OnLoad, and gm_801BA8DC_OnLoad as calls to gm_801A55C4().
Implemented gm_801B95D8_OnLoad with gm_SetGameSpeed(1.0F) followed by gm_801A55C4().

Implemented transition/cleanup helpers gm_801BEC80, gm_801BECA8, gm_801BECD0, and gm_801BEF84 using existing game-mode transition functions, lb_8001C550/lb_8001D164, gm_80172898/gm_80173754 checks, and lbMthp_8001F800.
Added dont_inline pragmas around gm_801BEFA4 and gm_801BEFC0, and changed gm_801BEFC0's argument type from s8 to int while still storing into gm_8049C178[1].

Implemented gm_801BF060(MinorScene*) as a debug-level-sensitive scene-data bitmask dispatcher, using flags 0x100, 0x1000, 0x400, and 0x800 to select transition IDs 0xE, 1, 7, or 6, and using a different path involving gm_80173EEC and gm_80172898(0x100) when not in high debug level.

Implemented gm_801BF728 as a randomized video/demo setup routine using HSD_Randi, vi0102_8031D020, un_8031D9E4, un_8031E110, un_8031F980, un_803204B0, globals such as un_804D6F3C/un_804D6F84/un_804D6FD8, and gm_801BEFA4/gm_801BEFC0.

Implemented gm_801BF834, gm_801BF85C, gm_801BF898, gm_801BF8B8, gm_801BF8D8, gm_801BF8F8, and gm_801BF920, covering scene transition, DVD preload-cache persistence clearing, MTHP calls, and MinorScene data writes.
Expanded includes in gm_1B03.c to bring in lbmthp, lb types, vi types, vi0102, vi0402, vi0501, vi1101, vi1201v1, and baselib random.
Updated many gm_1B03.h declarations from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to concrete void signatures, including numerous *_OnLoad functions and newly matched MinorScene callbacks.
Adjusted vi helper prototypes from s8 parameters to int parameters where the callers pass integer/random values, while writing into u8-backed storage.
Changed vi0102's un_804D6F60_t fields from s8 to u8 and updated vi0102_8031D020(int, int).
Changed vi0402's un_804D6F84 declaration to extern u8 un_804D6F84[8] and updated un_8031D9E4 to write u8 M2C_FIELDs.
Changed vi0501's un_804D6FA8 definition to extern in vi0501.c and updated un_8031E110 to int parameters and u8 M2C_FIELD writes.
Updated vi1101 and vi1201v1 setters to take int parameters and write u8 fields instead of s8 fields.
smart_moves: Matched many tiny functions by recognizing recurring game-mode scene patterns: OnInit functions set up gmMainLib scene slots, and corresponding OnLoad stubs often collapse to gm_801A55C4().
Kept storage widths byte-sized while widening public setter argument types to int, which better matches C calling convention/codegen for small integer arguments passed from HSD_Randi and constants.
Used #pragma dont_inline around very small byte setter functions to preserve the expected function boundaries instead of letting the compiler inline them and break matching.
Added the exact vi and library headers needed for newly matched calls instead of relying on broad UNK declarations.
Preserved M2C_FIELD usage where the underlying vi/global structure layout remains uncertain, but corrected signedness to u8 where byte data is being treated as unsigned indices/IDs.
Inserted PAD_STACK(4) in functions such as gm_801BF728 and gm_801BF85C where stack shape needed to match despite otherwise simple logic.
Updated headers in the same PR as the C matches, reducing future ambiguity around callback signatures and cross-module calls.
decomp_lessons: When matching scene callbacks, look for repeated OnLoad bodies across neighboring MinorScene entries; many may be identical one-call wrappers or one-call-plus-reset variants.
For byte-backed globals used as IDs or random selections, the storage type may need to be u8 even if function parameters are int for matching.
Small setter functions that only store one byte may require dont_inline pragmas to keep separate symbols matching the original binary.

Randomized setup code can require careful ordering of HSD_Randi calls and temporary variables; preserving evaluation order matters because each call advances RNG state and affects register allocation.
If a matched gm function starts calling vi helpers, fix the vi prototypes and extern declarations at the same time so the compiler has the same argument promotion and data-access behavior.
M2C_FIELD can be a practical transitional tactic for partially understood global layouts, but signedness and pointer type still affect generated loads/stores.

MinorScene callbacks often retrieve data through gm_801A4284 or gm_801A427C and then either inspect bitmasks or write small control values; these accessors are reusable anchors for future gm_1B03 matches.
Debug-level branches may expose alternate scene transition shortcuts; do not collapse them into one behavior even when release paths look simpler.
naming_conventions: Scene lifecycle callbacks use suffixes such as _OnInit, _OnLoad, and _OnUnload once their role is known.
Unidentified address-based symbols remain in the project style, e.g. gm_801BF060, gm_801BF728, un_8031D9E4, and vi0102_8031D020.
Global symbols preserve address-derived names such as gm_8049C178, gm_8049E548, gm_804D68C0, un_804D6F84, and un_804D6FD8.
Unknown struct fields remain offset-style, e.g. gmMainLib_804D3EE0->unk_6D0, unk_E50, unk_F90, unk_10D0, unk_A90, unk_BD0, unk_810, unk_1210, and unk_1350.
Header declarations retain address comments such as /* 1BF060 */ and /* 31D020 */ while replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS with concrete signatures when matched.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used #pragma push / #pragma dont_inline on / #pragma pop around gm_801BEFA4 and gm_801BEFC0 to prevent tiny setter functions from disappearing into callers.
Used PAD_STACK(4) in gm_801BF728 and gm_801BF85C to preserve stack layout/register allocation for matching.
Changed parameter types from s8 to int in vi0102_8031D020, un_8031D9E4, un_8031E110, un_8031F980, un_803204B0-related declarations, and gm_801BEFC0 to match call ABI while keeping u8 stores.
Changed M2C_FIELD casts from s8* to u8* for vi global byte fields to match unsigned byte stores/loads.
Introduced extern declarations for globals used by newly matched code rather than defining storage in the wrong translation unit.
Preserved temporary variables in gm_801BF728 for HSD_Randi results, likely to match register reuse and call ordering.
Matched gm_801BF060 using explicit nested if/else bitmask checks instead of abstracting the transition dispatch table, preserving branch structure.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR context.
The context reports one issue comment but provides an empty human_text_excerpt, so there is no evidence-grounded feedback to extract.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that continue matching remaining gm_1B03 stubs around gm_801B23F0, gm_801B24B4, gm_801B254C, gm_801BF128, gm_801BF3F8, and gm_801BF4DC.
Search for follow-up cleanup of un_804D6F84/un_804D6FA8/un_804D7004 structures to replace M2C_FIELD byte accesses with named fields.
Check whether the issue comment on PR 1712 contained merge automation only or any hidden reviewer note not included in the excerpt.
Search for other PRs using #pragma dont_inline on byte setter functions as a matching tactic.
Look for later naming of gm_804D68C0/gm_804D68C1 and gm_8049C178 state bytes.
